sources.reviewedCode:
# [Reader note] Entry point
# Start from the core routine and keep this mental model in view: Use partial work from each side instead of recomputing every view independently.
class Solution:
    def productExceptSelf(self, nums: List[int]) -> List[int]:
        # [Reader note] State setup
        # This setup stage creates the state needed for the invariant or partial result: Left-to-right and right-to-left accumulation, output construction, and disciplined state reuse.
        res = [1] * (len(nums))

        # [Reader note] Main pass
        # This is the main sweep where the implementation earns its efficiency instead of restarting work from scratch.
        for i in range(1, len(nums)):
            res[i] = res[i-1] * nums[i-1]
        postfix = 1
        for i in range(len(nums) - 1, -1, -1):
            res[i] *= postfix
            postfix *= nums[i]
        # [Reader note] Return
        # The final return exposes the result after the traversal and state updates have settled.
        return res

sources.reviewedCode:
# Time:  O(n)
# Space: O(1)

# [Reader note] Entry point
# Start from the core routine and keep this mental model in view: Use partial work from each side instead of recomputing every view independently.
class Solution(object):
    # @param {integer[]} nums
    # @return {integer[]}
    def productExceptSelf(self, nums):
        if not nums:
            return []

        # [Reader note] State setup
        # This setup stage creates the state needed for the invariant or partial result: Left-to-right and right-to-left accumulation, output construction, and disciplined state reuse.
        left_product = [1 for _ in xrange(len(nums))]
        for i in xrange(1, len(nums)):
            left_product[i] = left_product[i - 1] * nums[i - 1]

        right_product = 1
        for i in xrange(len(nums) - 2, -1, -1):
            right_product *= nums[i + 1]
            left_product[i] = left_product[i] * right_product

        # [Reader note] Return
        # The final return exposes the result after the traversal and state updates have settled.
        return left_product

sources.reviewedCode:
# [Reader note] Entry point
# Start from the core routine and keep this mental model in view: Use partial work from each side instead of recomputing every view independently.
class Solution:
    def productExceptSelf(self, nums: List[int]) -> List[int]:
        # [Reader note] State setup
        # This setup stage creates the state needed for the invariant or partial result: Left-to-right and right-to-left accumulation, output construction, and disciplined state reuse.
        n = len(nums)
        ans = [0] * n
        left = right = 1
        # [Reader note] Main pass
        # This is the main sweep where the implementation earns its efficiency instead of restarting work from scratch.
        for i, x in enumerate(nums):
            ans[i] = left
            left *= x
        for i in range(n - 1, -1, -1):
            ans[i] *= right
            right *= nums[i]
        # [Reader note] Return
        # The final return exposes the result after the traversal and state updates have settled.
        return ans
